Property.title: Property
Property.description: Represents a single rental property managed by the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Property entity.

address.description: The full postal address of the property.

type.description: The type of dwelling (e.g., Flat, House, HMO).

status.description: The current occupancy or maintenance status of the property.

numberOfBedrooms.description: The total number of bedrooms in the property.

numberOfBathrooms.description: The total number of bathrooms in the property.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the property record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the property record was last updated.

Tenant.title: Tenant
Tenant.description: Represents an individual tenant who occupies or has occupied a property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tenant entity.

name.description: The full name of the tenant.

email.description: The email address of the tenant.

phoneNumber.description: The primary contact telephone number for the tenant.

notes.description: General notes or remarks about the tenant.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the tenant record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the tenant record was last updated.

Tenancy.title: Tenancy
Tenancy.description: Represents an agreement between a tenant(s) and a property for a specific period.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tenancy entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property associated with this tenancy. (Relationship: Property 1:N Tenancy)

tenantIds.description: References to Tenants associated with this tenancy. (Relationship: Tenancy N:N Tenant)

startDate.description: The official start date of the tenancy agreement.

endDate.description: The official end date of the tenancy agreement.

notes.description: Specific notes or conditions related to this tenancy agreement.

isActive.description: Indicates if the tenancy is currently active based on its start and end dates.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the tenancy record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the tenancy record was last updated.

MaintenanceRequest.title: MaintenanceRequest
MaintenanceRequest.description: Represents a request or issue related to property maintenance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MaintenanceRequest entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property where maintenance is required. (Relationship: Property 1:N MaintenanceRequest)

description.description: A detailed description of the maintenance issue.

urgency.description: The level of urgency for the maintenance request (e.g., Low, Medium, High).

status.description: The current status of the maintenance request (e.g., Open, In Progress, Completed, Cancelled).

reportedAt.description: Timestamp when the maintenance request was reported.

scheduledDate.description: Optional timestamp for when maintenance work is scheduled.

completedAt.description: Optional timestamp when the maintenance request was completed.

notes.description: Internal notes regarding the maintenance resolution or progress.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the maintenance request record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the maintenance request record was last updated.

Inspection.title: Inspection
Inspection.description: Represents a scheduled or completed inspection for a property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Inspection entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property for which the inspection is scheduled. (Relationship: Property 1:N Inspection)

scheduledDateTime.description: The scheduled date and time for the inspection.

type.description: The type of inspection (e.g., Move-in, Mid-term, Annual).

status.description: The current status of the inspection (e.g., Scheduled, Completed, Cancelled).

notes.description: Notes or findings from the inspection.

completedAt.description: Optional timestamp when the inspection was completed.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the inspection record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the inspection record was last updated.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents a digital document or image associated with properties, tenancies, or related activities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

propertyId.description: Optional reference to the Property this document is associated with. (Relationship: Property 1:N Document)

tenancyId.description: Optional reference to the Tenancy this document is associated with. (Relationship: Tenancy 1:N Document)

maintenanceRequestId.description: Optional reference to the MaintenanceRequest this document is associated with (e.g., invoice, quote). (Relationship: MaintenanceRequest 1:N Document)

inspectionId.description: Optional reference to the Inspection this document is associated with (e.g., report). (Relationship: Inspection 1:N Document)

filename.description: The original filename of the uploaded document.

fileType.description: The MIME type of the file (e.g., 'image/jpeg', 'application/pdf').

fileUrl.description: The URL or path where the document is stored.

description.description: A brief description or context for the document.

category.description: Categorization of the document's purpose (e.g., PropertyImage, TenancyAgreement, Invoice).

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp when the document was uploaded.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the document record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the document record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores details for each rental property managed by a specific user (landlord). The `ownerId` in the path ensures authorization independence and path-based ownership.

params.name: ownerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (landlord) who owns and manages these properties.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific property.

definition.description: Stores master records for tenants managed by a specific user. Placed directly under the user's path to enable simple collection queries for all of a user's tenants, resolving collectionGroup query issues. The `ownerId` in the path ensures authorization independence and path-based ownership.

params.name: ownerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (landlord) who manages these tenants.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific tenant.

definition.description: Stores details of tenancy agreements, linking specific tenants to a property. Inherits ownership from the `ownerId` and `propertyId` in its hierarchical path, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: ownerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (landlord) who owns this tenancy.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the property associated with this tenancy.

params.name: tenancyId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific tenancy agreement.

definition.description: Stores maintenance requests specific to a property. Inherits ownership from the `ownerId` and `propertyId` in its hierarchical path, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: ownerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (landlord) who owns this maintenance request.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the property to which this maintenance request pertains.

params.name: maintenanceRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific maintenance request.

definition.description: Stores records of property inspections. Inherits ownership from the `ownerId` and `propertyId` in its hierarchical path, ensuring authorization independence.

params.name: ownerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (landlord) who owns this inspection record.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the property for which this inspection is scheduled/completed.

params.name: inspectionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific inspection record.

definition.description: Stores all digital documents for a user, such as property images, agreements, etc. Documents contain foreign keys (propertyId, tenancyId, etc.) to link them to related entities. The `ownerId` in the path ensures authorization independence and path-based ownership, enabling easy `list` operations for a user's documents.

params.name: ownerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (landlord) who owns these documents.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific document.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to adhere strictly to the CORE DESIGN PRINCIPLES and DESIGN STRATEGY MANDATES, prioritizing security, scalability, and debuggability for a single-landlord application. The primary strategy employed is **Path-Based Ownership** and **Structural Segregation**.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL)** is achieved by rooting all user-specific data under `/users/{ownerId}`. This places the `ownerId` (which corresponds to `request.auth.uid`) directly in the path for every collection and subcollection. Consequently, Firestore Security Rules will not require any `get()` operations to verify ownership, making rules atomic, simple (`if request.auth.uid == ownerId`), and highly performant. For example, a `Property` document at `users/{ownerId}/properties/{propertyId}` is implicitly owned by `ownerId` from the path, removing any dependency on parent document data for authorization.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters)** are fully supported because the data is organized by ownership. When a user requests a `list` operation on any of their top-level collections (e.g., `/users/{ownerId}/properties`, `/users/{ownerId}/tenants`, `/users/{ownerId}/documents`), the `ownerId` in the path perfectly aligns with `request.auth.uid`. This means the security rules can grant `allow list` access based solely on the path's `ownerId` matching the authenticated user's UID, ensuring that only the requesting user's data is returned, without the rules needing to act as filters.

**Addressing the `tenants (collectionGroup)` error:** The original error indicates an attempt to perform a `list` operation using a collection group query on 'tenants'. This typically happens when 'tenant' subcollections are nested under various parent documents (e.g., `properties/{propertyId}/tenants/{tenantId}`), and the client attempts to query all tenants across all properties. My recommended structure addresses this by creating a dedicated, user-scoped collection for `Tenants` at `users/{ownerId}/tenants/{tenantId}`. This design makes a collection group query on 'tenants' unnecessary for a single landlord's data. Instead, the application should perform a standard collection query (e.g., `firestore.collection('users').doc(auth.uid).collection('tenants').get()`), which is easily secured by the path-based authorization. This resolves the permission error by aligning the data structure with a straightforward collection query that adheres to the user's explicit ownership.

**Structural Segregation** is maintained by ensuring that documents within any given collection (e.g., `properties`, `tenants`, `documents`) all share the same security posture: they are owned by the `ownerId` in the path. Even for `Document` entities, which can be related to multiple other entities, they are placed in a user-scoped collection (`users/{ownerId}/documents`) where their authorization is simply tied to the `ownerId`, rather than attempting to inherit complex, potentially conflicting permissions from multiple possible parent entities. Relationships between entities are managed through explicit foreign key IDs (e.g., `propertyId`, `tenantIds` in `Tenancy`, `propertyId` in `MaintenanceRequest` and `Inspection`, and various `_Id` fields in `Document`), which are used for querying and data integrity, not for authorization checks by security rules.
